meta.odds_source: The Odds API — Unibet, TAB, Betfair AU
meta.knockout_note: Knockout stage data will populate automatically when the API provides match stage data

commentary.title: T Rankin: The Netherlands Carry a Passenger Manifest
commentary.body:
Croatia are eliminated, which reduces T Rankin's portfolio to three functioning assets and one passenger. Ghana remain an intriguing if unreliable wildcard, Tunisia a side that has not yet distinguished itself from the tournament wallpaper. The Netherlands, however, are the story here — a genuinely dangerous knockout-round side capable of going deep into the bracket and dragging T Rankin's Win% with them. At 6.30%, the ranking undersells the Dutch.

The mathematics are unforgiving but not hopeless. Three teams still operating, one with genuine pedigree, and the knockout rounds are where Win% moves in lurches rather than increments. If the Netherlands advance with conviction, T Rankin's position at rank 8 could look considerably more embarrassing to those above them than it currently does to T Rankin.

commentary.title: Hankin: Most Points on the Board, Least Credit in the Room
commentary.body:
Nineteen points. Six wins. One draw. Two losses. By any conventional measure of footballing performance, Hankin's teams are the most productive collection in this sweepstake. Mexico dispatched Czech Republic 3-0 on 25 June with the sort of efficiency that suggests they intend to be inconvenient for some time yet. Norway, Ivory Coast, and Mexico are all performing well above the expectations baked into their market odds. Saudi Arabia are gone, but three teams punching above their weight is a better situation than most participants can claim.

The 4.54% Win% is the cruel arithmetic of sweepstake life — you can win every group game and still be priced like a side effect. The market does not reward group-stage industry; it rewards the narrow probability of lifting the thing. Hankin's case for the defence is strong. The verdict, unfortunately, is still being written by someone else's odds compiler.

commentary.title: Varcoe: Unbeaten, Underrated, and Still Nobody's Favourite
commentary.body:
On 25 June, Varcoe achieved something genuinely unusual: Switzerland beat Canada 2-1, meaning Varcoe watched two of his own teams contest a match, collected the points from the winner, and absorbed the loss from the loser, finishing the afternoon precisely as conflicted as he started it. The net effect was positive — Switzerland are through, Canada less so — but the internal sweepstake accounting requires a composure most people reserve for divorce proceedings. Japan, Switzerland, and DR Congo remain active and have collectively produced an unbeaten record across all their games.

At 3.59% and rank 10, the market remains stubbornly unimpressed. A GD of plus-12 is the best in the sweepstake, which suggests Varcoe's teams are not merely surviving but doing so with a degree of contempt for the opposition. Japan in particular carry knockout credibility. The quiet overachiever label is accurate, though it will feel considerably less quiet if the odds begin to move.

exec_summary.pull_quote: Galbraith collects points on two continents while Silk watches Qatar concede three.
exec_summary.paragraph_1: The defining results of June 25th cut in two directions. Galbraith's portfolio delivered on both fronts: Morocco's 4–2 dismantling of Kenna's Haiti and South Africa's narrow 1–0 over an unowned South Korea pushed Galbraith to 17 points, consolidating fourth place with genuine upward momentum. Morris enjoyed a frictionless afternoon, with Brazil routing his own Scotland 3–1 — a result that costs him nothing and earns him plenty. Silk, meanwhile, absorbed a punishing 3–1 defeat via Bosnia-Herzegovina, a team he does not own eliminating points he did.
exec_summary.paragraph_2: Friday's early fixtures present the sharper edges. Kenna's Curaçao face Hankin's Ivory Coast in a Group E fixture where defeat would further depress a record already reading W2-D4-L9. P Rankin holds Germany and Sweden simultaneously, offering unusual insulation in Group E and F respectively, though Ecuador and Japan represent credible threats from Crowle and Varcoe. T Rankin's peculiar ownership of both Tunisia and the Netherlands means Group F's 9am kick-off is, for him, an exercise in hoping one half of his portfolio underperforms gracefully.

trivia_questions.question: Which country won the first ever FIFA World Cup in 1930?
trivia_questions.explanation: Uruguay hosted and won the inaugural World Cup in 1930, beating Argentina 4-2 in the final.

trivia_questions.question: Who holds the all-time record for most goals scored in World Cup history?
trivia_questions.explanation: Miroslav Klose scored 16 goals across four World Cups (2002–2014). Messi equalled that record at WC2026.

trivia_questions.question: At the 2026 World Cup, which player scored a hat-trick to equal the all-time World Cup scoring record?
trivia_questions.explanation: Messi scored three goals against Algeria to reach 16 World Cup goals, equalling Klose's all-time record.

trivia_questions.question: How many nations co-hosted the 2026 FIFA World Cup?
trivia_questions.explanation: The 2026 World Cup was jointly hosted by the USA, Canada and Mexico — the first World Cup hosted by three nations.

trivia_questions.question: How many teams competed in the expanded 2026 FIFA World Cup?
trivia_questions.explanation: 2026 expanded to 48 teams for the first time, up from the 32-team format used since 1998.

trivia_questions.question: Which country beat Qatar 6-0 at the 2026 World Cup, with a Jonathan David hat-trick?
trivia_questions.explanation: Co-host Canada demolished Qatar 6-0 with Jonathan David scoring a hat-trick.

trivia_questions.question: Brazil has won the most World Cups. How many titles do they have?
trivia_questions.explanation: Brazil have won five World Cups: 1958, 1962, 1970, 1994 and 2002.

trivia_questions.question: Which team did France beat in the 2018 World Cup final?
trivia_questions.explanation: France beat Croatia 4-2 in the 2018 World Cup final in Moscow.

trivia_questions.question: In the 2022 World Cup final, what was the score after extra time?
trivia_questions.explanation: The 2022 final finished 3-3 after extra time, with Argentina winning 4-2 on penalties.

trivia_questions.question: Which Spanish teenager was one of the most watched players at the 2026 World Cup?
trivia_questions.explanation: Lamine Yamal, aged 17, was one of Spain's key players at the 2026 tournament.

trivia_questions.question: Which team won 2026 World Cup Group C, beating Scotland and drawing with Brazil?
trivia_questions.explanation: Morocco won Group C with a 1-0 win over Scotland and a draw against Brazil.

trivia_questions.question: The USA topped their 2026 World Cup group. Which group were they in?
trivia_questions.explanation: The USA were in Group D alongside Paraguay, Australia and Turkey.

trivia_questions.question: Which country has won back-to-back World Cup titles — the only nation to do so?
trivia_questions.explanation: Brazil won back-to-back World Cups in 1958 and 1962.

trivia_questions.question: Who scored the famous 'Hand of God' goal at the 1986 World Cup?
trivia_questions.explanation: Diego Maradona scored the 'Hand of God' goal against England in the 1986 quarter-final.

trivia_questions.question: What is the highest score in a single World Cup match?
trivia_questions.explanation: Hungary beat El Salvador 10-1 in the 1982 World Cup.

trivia_questions.question: Which 2026 World Cup group did France and Norway both play in?
trivia_questions.explanation: France and Norway were both in Group I, along with Senegal and Iraq.

trivia_questions.question: How many goals did the Netherlands score against Sweden at the 2026 World Cup?
trivia_questions.explanation: The Netherlands beat Sweden 5-1 in Group F.

trivia_questions.question: Which World Cup saw VAR used for the first time?
trivia_questions.explanation: VAR was introduced at the 2018 World Cup in Russia.

trivia_questions.question: Who won the Golden Boot at the 2022 World Cup?
trivia_questions.explanation: Kylian Mbappé won the Golden Boot at Qatar 2022 with 8 goals.

trivia_questions.question: Who scored the winning penalty for Argentina in the 2022 World Cup final shootout?
trivia_questions.explanation: Gonzalo Montiel scored the decisive penalty to win Argentina the 2022 World Cup.

trivia_questions.question: Which team knocked out defending champions Germany in the 2018 World Cup group stage?
trivia_questions.explanation: South Korea beat Germany 2-0, eliminating the defending champions.

trivia_questions.question: France beat Senegal 3-1 at the 2026 World Cup. Who scored twice in that match?
trivia_questions.explanation: Kylian Mbappé scored twice against Senegal, becoming France's all-time leading scorer.

trivia_questions.question: What year did Pelé score his first World Cup goal, aged just 17?
trivia_questions.explanation: Pelé burst onto the world stage at the 1958 World Cup in Sweden.

trivia_questions.question: Which nation hosted the 2010 FIFA World Cup — the first ever on African soil?
trivia_questions.explanation: South Africa hosted the 2010 World Cup, the first tournament on African soil.

trivia_questions.question: Which 2026 World Cup group had Argentina, Austria, Algeria and Jordan?
trivia_questions.explanation: Argentina were in Group J alongside Austria, Algeria and Jordan.

trivia_questions.question: Which goalkeeper made two penalty saves to help Argentina win the 2022 World Cup final shootout?
trivia_questions.explanation: Emiliano 'Dibu' Martínez made two saves in the 2022 final shootout.

trivia_questions.question: Which iconic stadium hosted the opening match of the 2026 World Cup?
trivia_questions.explanation: The 2026 World Cup opened at the Estadio Azteca in Mexico City.

trivia_questions.question: Which team has appeared in the most World Cup finals without ever winning?
trivia_questions.explanation: The Netherlands have appeared in three World Cup finals (1974, 1978, 2010) without winning.

trivia_questions.question: How old was Lamine Yamal when he played at the 2026 World Cup?
trivia_questions.explanation: Lamine Yamal was 17 years old during the 2026 World Cup.

trivia_questions.question: Germany beat which host nation 7-1 in the 2014 World Cup semi-final?
trivia_questions.explanation: Germany's 7-1 destruction of host Brazil in 2014 — the 'Mineirazo' — is legendary.

trivia_questions.question: Which country won the 2022 FIFA World Cup?
trivia_questions.explanation: Argentina won their third World Cup in Qatar 2022, with Lionel Messi lifting the trophy.

trivia_questions.question: How many World Cups did Lionel Messi participate in throughout his career?
trivia_questions.explanation: Messi played in five World Cups: 2006, 2010, 2014, 2018 and 2022.

trivia_questions.question: Which nation made a surprise run to the 2022 World Cup semi-finals — the first African team ever to do so?
trivia_questions.explanation: Morocco made history by reaching the 2022 World Cup semi-finals.

trivia_questions.question: Which player won the Golden Ball (best player) at the 2022 World Cup?
trivia_questions.explanation: Lionel Messi won the Golden Ball award at Qatar 2022.

trivia_questions.question: How many times has Germany won the World Cup?
trivia_questions.explanation: Germany (including West Germany) have won four World Cups: 1954, 1974, 1990 and 2014.

trivia_questions.question: Which country lost the 2010 World Cup final to Spain?
trivia_questions.explanation: The Netherlands lost 1-0 to Spain in the 2010 final, with Andres Iniesta scoring the winner.

trivia_questions.question: Who scored the winning goal for Spain in the 2010 World Cup final?
trivia_questions.explanation: Andres Iniesta scored in extra time to give Spain their first ever World Cup title.

trivia_questions.question: Which goalkeeper famously saved penalties to make Cape Verde's 0-0 draw with Spain at WC2026 possible?
trivia_questions.explanation: Vozinha, a 40-year-old keeper playing in the Portuguese second division, made extraordinary saves to hold Spain scoreless.

trivia_questions.question: What did Zinedine Zidane do in the 2006 World Cup final that made global headlines?
options: Was sent off for a headbutt
trivia_questions.explanation: Zidane was sent off for headbutting Marco Materazzi in extra time of the 2006 final — his last ever professional game.

trivia_questions.question: Which country hosted the 2014 World Cup?
trivia_questions.explanation: Brazil hosted the 2014 World Cup and was famously beaten 7-1 by Germany in the semi-final.

trivia_questions.question: Which player is known by the nickname 'O Fenômeno' (The Phenomenon)?
trivia_questions.explanation: Ronaldo Nazário — the Brazilian striker — was nicknamed 'O Fenômeno'.

trivia_questions.question: How many goals did Ronaldo (Brazil) score in the 2002 World Cup final against Germany?
trivia_questions.explanation: Ronaldo scored twice in the 2002 final as Brazil beat Germany 2-0.

trivia_questions.question: Which team did Italy beat in the 1982 World Cup final?
trivia_questions.explanation: Italy beat West Germany 3-1 in the 1982 final. Paolo Rossi was top scorer with 6 goals.

trivia_questions.question: Which player scored a hat-trick in the 1966 World Cup final for England?
trivia_questions.explanation: Geoff Hurst scored a hat-trick in the 1966 final against West Germany — the only hat-trick in a World Cup final.

trivia_questions.question: Which team scored the fastest goal in World Cup history, in just 11 seconds?
trivia_questions.explanation: Hakan Şükür scored for Turkey after just 11 seconds against South Korea in the 2002 third-place playoff.

trivia_questions.question: Which player has appeared in the most World Cup finals across their career?
trivia_questions.explanation: Cafu of Brazil appeared in three World Cup finals (1994, 1998, 2002), winning twice.

trivia_questions.question: Just Fontaine holds the record for most goals scored at a single World Cup. How many did he score?
trivia_questions.explanation: Just Fontaine of France scored 13 goals at the 1958 World Cup — a record that still stands.

trivia_questions.question: Which nation won the World Cup without losing a single game across the entire tournament in 1970?
trivia_questions.explanation: Brazil won the 1970 World Cup winning all six matches — widely regarded as the greatest World Cup team ever.

trivia_questions.question: England won their only World Cup in which year?
trivia_questions.explanation: England won their only World Cup in 1966, beating West Germany 4-2 in the final at Wembley.

trivia_questions.question: Which team has never missed a World Cup since they first qualified?
trivia_questions.explanation: Brazil are the only team to have qualified for every single FIFA World Cup since 1930.

trivia_questions.question: How many times has Argentina won the World Cup?
trivia_questions.explanation: Argentina have won the World Cup three times: 1978, 1986 and 2022.

trivia_questions.question: Which player was voted the best goalkeeper (Golden Glove) at the 2022 World Cup?
trivia_questions.explanation: Emiliano Martínez won the Golden Glove at the 2022 World Cup.

trivia_questions.question: Which player is known as 'La Pulga' (The Flea)?
trivia_questions.explanation: Lionel Messi is nicknamed 'La Pulga' due to his small stature and quick, agile playing style.

trivia_questions.question: Which player scored the most goals at the 2026 World Cup group stage?
trivia_questions.explanation: Lionel Messi led the group stage scoring charts with his hat-trick against Algeria plus a goal against Austria.

trivia_questions.question: Which World Cup was the first to be held outside Europe or the Americas?
trivia_questions.explanation: The 2002 World Cup in Japan and South Korea was the first held in Asia.

trivia_questions.question: Which player scored in the 2022 World Cup semi-final AND the final for Argentina?
trivia_questions.explanation: Julian Alvarez scored twice vs Croatia in the semi-final and once in the final.

trivia_questions.question: Mexico co-hosted the 2026 World Cup. They also hosted the World Cup in which two previous years?
trivia_questions.explanation: Mexico hosted the World Cup in 1970 and 1986, making 2026 their third time hosting.

trivia_questions.question: Which country qualified for the 2026 World Cup knockouts by topping Group E?
trivia_questions.explanation: Germany topped Group E with wins including their famous 7-1 win over Curaçao.

trivia_questions.question: Which player won the Young Player Award at the 2022 World Cup?
trivia_questions.explanation: Enzo Fernández of Argentina won the Young Player Award at Qatar 2022.

trivia_questions.question: How many own goals were scored at the 2022 World Cup in Qatar — a new record?
trivia_questions.explanation: There were 9 own goals at the 2022 World Cup in Qatar, a new tournament record.

trivia_questions.question: Which player scored the fastest hat-trick in World Cup history?
trivia_questions.explanation: Laszlo Kiss of Hungary scored three times in 7 minutes against El Salvador in 1982.

trivia_questions.question: Which Italian player headbutted Zidane in the 2006 World Cup final?
trivia_questions.explanation: Marco Materazzi provoked Zinedine Zidane into a headbutt in the 2006 final.

trivia_questions.question: Which team has scored the most goals in a single World Cup tournament?
trivia_questions.explanation: Hungary scored 27 goals in just 5 matches at the 1954 World Cup.

trivia_questions.question: Belgium drew both their 2026 World Cup group games. Who did they draw with?
trivia_questions.explanation: Belgium drew 1-1 with Egypt and 0-0 with Iran in their Group G games.

trivia_questions.question: Which player scored twice for Cody Gakpo's Netherlands in the 5-1 win over Sweden at WC2026?
trivia_questions.explanation: Cody Gakpo scored twice in the Netherlands' 5-1 demolition of Sweden.

trivia_questions.question: Japan beat Tunisia 4-0 at the 2026 World Cup. Which player scored twice for Japan?
trivia_questions.explanation: Nakamura scored twice as Japan beat Tunisia 4-0 in Group F.

trivia_questions.question: Scotland beat Haiti 1-0 at the 2026 World Cup. What was significant about this?
options: It was Scotland's first WC win in 24 years
It was Scotland's first WC win ever
It qualified Scotland for the knockouts
trivia_questions.explanation: Scotland beat Haiti 1-0 — their first World Cup win in 24 years.

trivia_questions.question: Erling Haaland scored twice for Norway against which team at the 2026 World Cup?
trivia_questions.explanation: Erling Haaland scored twice for Norway in their group stage win over Iraq.

trivia_questions.question: Which African nation beat Argentina 1-0 in a famous 1990 World Cup group stage upset?
trivia_questions.explanation: Cameroon beat defending champions Argentina 1-0 in the 1990 opening match.

trivia_questions.question: Which player is the youngest ever to score at a World Cup?
trivia_questions.explanation: Pelé scored at 17 years and 239 days old at the 1958 World Cup.

trivia_questions.question: Uruguay drew 2-2 with Cape Verde at the 2026 World Cup. What was surprising about this result?
options: Uruguay were 2-0 up at half time
It was Uruguay's first draw ever
trivia_questions.explanation: Uruguay led 2-0 but Cape Verde equalised — dropping two crucial points.

trivia_questions.question: How many goals did Germany score across their two 2026 World Cup group stage games?
trivia_questions.explanation: Germany scored 9 goals: 7-1 vs Curaçao and 2-1 vs Ivory Coast.

trivia_questions.question: Which player scored twice for Brazil against Haiti at the 2026 World Cup?
trivia_questions.explanation: Vinicius Jr. scored twice as Brazil beat Haiti 3-0.

trivia_questions.question: Which team did Morocco beat 1-0 to win 2026 World Cup Group C?
trivia_questions.explanation: Morocco beat Scotland 1-0 in their Group C clash, helping them top the group.

trivia_questions.question: Which player scored a stunning bicycle kick at the 2018 World Cup that was voted goal of the tournament?
trivia_questions.explanation: Benjamin Pavard scored a stunning right-foot volley for France against Argentina in 2018 — voted goal of the tournament.

trivia_questions.question: How many red cards were shown at the 2022 World Cup?
trivia_questions.explanation: Six red cards were shown at the 2022 World Cup in Qatar.

trivia_questions.question: Which team knocked out the holders Brazil at the 2010 World Cup quarter-finals?
trivia_questions.explanation: The Netherlands eliminated Brazil 2-1 in the 2010 quarter-finals.

trivia_questions.question: Which team was in the same 2026 World Cup group as Netherlands, Japan and Sweden?
trivia_questions.explanation: Tunisia were in Group F at the 2026 World Cup alongside Netherlands, Japan and Sweden.

trivia_questions.question: Ecuador drew 0-0 with which team at the 2026 World Cup?
trivia_questions.explanation: Ecuador drew 0-0 with Curaçao in Group E of the 2026 World Cup.

trivia_questions.question: Which country's goalkeeper Dayne St. Clair kept a clean sheet in a 6-0 win at the 2026 World Cup?
trivia_questions.explanation: Dayne St. Clair was Canada's first-choice goalkeeper in their 6-0 win over Qatar.

trivia_questions.question: Which player won the 2022 World Cup's best goalkeeper award after saving two penalties in the final shootout?
trivia_questions.explanation: Emiliano 'Dibu' Martínez won the Golden Glove after saving two penalties in the final shootout.

trivia_questions.question: Which team did Australia beat in the 2022 World Cup Round of 16 — their best World Cup result at the time?
trivia_questions.explanation: Australia beat Denmark 1-0 in the 2022 Round of 16.

trivia_questions.question: Germany beat Ivory Coast 2-1 at the 2026 World Cup. In which Canadian city was this match played?
trivia_questions.explanation: Germany beat Ivory Coast 2-1 in Toronto at the 2026 World Cup.

trivia_questions.question: What was the score of the 2022 World Cup final after 90 minutes?
trivia_questions.explanation: Argentina led 2-0 after 90 minutes before Mbappé scored twice in two minutes to equalise, making it 2-2.

trivia_questions.question: Norway beat Iraq 4-1 at the 2026 World Cup. In which US city?
trivia_questions.explanation: Norway's 4-1 win over Iraq was played in Seattle.

trivia_questions.question: Which player scored the most goals at a single Women's World Cup?
trivia_questions.explanation: Marta scored 17 goals across multiple World Cups, with her best single tournament being 2007 (7 goals).

trivia_questions.question: Which 2026 World Cup group was known as the toughest, with Argentina, Austria, Algeria and Jordan?
trivia_questions.explanation: Group J — containing Argentina, Austria, Algeria and Jordan — was considered one of the tournament's toughest groups.

trivia_questions.question: Egypt beat New Zealand 3-1 in the 2026 World Cup. Which group did they play in?
trivia_questions.explanation: Egypt and New Zealand were in Group G at the 2026 World Cup.

trivia_questions.question: Paraguay beat Turkey 1-0 at the 2026 World Cup in which group?
trivia_questions.explanation: Paraguay beat Turkey 1-0 in Group D of the 2026 World Cup.

trivia_questions.question: Spain beat Saudi Arabia 4-0 at the 2026 World Cup. After a disappointing opening result, what was Spain's first game result?
trivia_questions.explanation: Spain drew 0-0 with Cape Verde in their opening game before bouncing back to beat Saudi Arabia 4-0.

trivia_questions.question: Which legendary striker scored 15 World Cup goals across three tournaments for Brazil before Ronaldo broke his record?
trivia_questions.explanation: Pelé scored 12 World Cup goals across 1958, 1962 and 1966 — not 15. Ronaldo held the record with 15 before Klose broke it.

trivia_questions.question: Which country qualified from 2026 World Cup Group B alongside Canada?
trivia_questions.explanation: Switzerland qualified from Group B alongside Canada, with both teams advancing.

trivia_questions.question: France's Kylian Mbappé became his country's all-time leading scorer at the 2026 World Cup against which team?
trivia_questions.explanation: Mbappé scored twice against Senegal to become France's all-time leading scorer.

trivia_questions.question: Which African team surprisingly beat Spain 2-1 in the 2022 World Cup Round of 16?
trivia_questions.explanation: Morocco beat Spain on penalties in the 2022 Round of 16 after a 0-0 draw — one of the biggest shocks of the tournament.

trivia_questions.question: Scotland lost to Morocco in the 2026 World Cup. What was the final score?
trivia_questions.explanation: Morocco beat Scotland 1-0 in their Group C clash.

trivia_questions.question: Which country's team plays their home games at the Maracanã stadium in Rio de Janeiro?
trivia_questions.explanation: The Maracanã is Brazil's most famous football stadium, home to the Brazilian national team.

trivia_questions.question: At the 2026 World Cup, which group had Belgium, Egypt, Iran and New Zealand?
trivia_questions.explanation: Belgium, Egypt, Iran and New Zealand were all in Group G.

trivia_questions.question: Which 2022 World Cup star scored against both Japan in the group stage and Brazil in the quarter-final for South Korea?
trivia_questions.explanation: Hwang Hee-chan scored the winner against Portugal and a crucial goal against Brazil for South Korea in 2022.

trivia_questions.question: Brazil beat which team 3-0 to qualify from Group C at the 2026 World Cup?
trivia_questions.explanation: Brazil beat Haiti 3-0 in their Group C game to advance to the knockout rounds.

trivia_questions.question: Which World Cup final venue is in East Rutherford, New Jersey, USA?
trivia_questions.explanation: MetLife Stadium in East Rutherford, NJ, is scheduled to host the 2026 World Cup final.

trivia_questions.question: Spain won the 2010 World Cup — the first time they had won it. True or false?
trivia_questions.explanation: Spain's 2010 triumph was their first and only World Cup victory.

trivia_questions.question: Which 2026 World Cup group had USA, Paraguay, Australia and Turkey?
trivia_questions.explanation: The USA, Paraguay, Australia and Turkey were in Group D.

trivia_questions.question: Norway qualified from the 2026 World Cup group stage ahead of which team in their group?
trivia_questions.explanation: Norway finished second in Group I behind France, with Senegal third and Iraq eliminated.

trivia_questions.question: Which French player became their all-time top scorer at the 2026 World Cup?
trivia_questions.explanation: Kylian Mbappé surpassed Olivier Giroud to become France's all-time top scorer during the 2026 World Cup.

trivia_questions.question: In what year was FIFA founded?
trivia_questions.explanation: FIFA (Fédération Internationale de Football Association) was founded in Paris on 21 May 1904.

trivia_questions.question: Which World Cup had the infamous 'Battle of Santiago' — one of the most violent matches ever played?
trivia_questions.explanation: The 'Battle of Santiago' between Chile and Italy at the 1962 World Cup saw two sendings-off and multiple fights in a notorious match.

trivia_questions.question: Which team won the inaugural FIFA World Cup in 1930 without a single defeat?
trivia_questions.explanation: Uruguay went unbeaten through the 1930 World Cup, winning all four of their matches.

trivia_questions.question: Which Australian city would have fans watching the 2026 World Cup final at 6am local time?
trivia_questions.explanation: Sydney (AEDT) is UTC+10 in winter, so a 4pm New York kickoff would be 6am the following morning in Sydney.

trivia_questions.question: Which player scored an extraordinary chip goal for Nigeria against Spain at the 1998 World Cup?
trivia_questions.explanation: Jay-Jay Okocha scored a stunning chip against Spain at France '98 in a classic moment.

trivia_questions.question: How many goals did Spain score in the 2026 World Cup group stage?
trivia_questions.explanation: Spain scored 4 goals in the group stage: 0 vs Cape Verde, 4 vs Saudi Arabia.

trivia_questions.question: At the 2026 World Cup, Group K had Portugal, Colombia, DR Congo and which other team?
trivia_questions.explanation: Group K consisted of Portugal, Colombia, DR Congo and Uzbekistan.

trivia_questions.question: Which country has hosted the most World Cups?
trivia_questions.explanation: Mexico hosted the World Cup in 1970, 1986 and 2026 — more times than any other country.

trivia_questions.question: Ecuador drew 0-0 with Curaçao at WC2026. Which sweepstake participant owns Ecuador?
trivia_questions.explanation: Crowle holds Ecuador in the WC2026 sweepstake.

trivia_questions.question: In the sweepstake, which participant owns both Brazil and Uruguay?
trivia_questions.explanation: Morris owns both Brazil and Uruguay in the WC2026 sweepstake.

trivia_questions.question: Which sweepstake participant owns France — the tournament favourite?
trivia_questions.explanation: Cronan owns France, giving them the highest win probability in the sweepstake.

trivia_questions.question: Which participant owns Argentina AND Morocco in the sweepstake?
trivia_questions.explanation: Galbraith owns Argentina and Morocco — two teams who performed well in the group stage.

trivia_questions.question: Which sweepstake participant owns the most teams?
trivia_questions.explanation: Kenna owns 8 teams — Spain, Senegal, South Korea, Egypt, Jordan, New Zealand, Curaçao and Haiti.

trivia_questions.question: Varcoe owns Japan, Switzerland, DR Congo and which other team?
trivia_questions.explanation: Varcoe owns Canada, who famously beat Qatar 6-0 at the 2026 World Cup.

trivia_questions.question: Which participant owns Germany — their flagship asset who qualified from Group E?
trivia_questions.explanation: P Rankin owns Germany in the WC2026 sweepstake.

trivia_questions.question: Which sweepstake participant owns the Netherlands, who beat Sweden 5-1?
trivia_questions.explanation: T Rankin owns the Netherlands in the WC2026 sweepstake.

trivia_questions.question: Hankin owns Norway, Mexico, Ivory Coast and which other team?
trivia_questions.explanation: Hankin owns Saudi Arabia — though they were eliminated in the group stage after Spain beat them 4-0.

trivia_questions.question: Which sweepstake participant owns England and the USA?
trivia_questions.explanation: Silk owns England and USA — both strong performers at the 2026 World Cup.

trivia_questions.question: Same owns Portugal, Colombia, Paraguay and which other team?
trivia_questions.explanation: Same owns Cape Verde — the team that famously drew 0-0 with Spain despite heavy pressure.

trivia_questions.question: Which participant owns Belgium, Ecuador, Bosnia and Uzbekistan?
trivia_questions.explanation: Crowle owns Belgium, Ecuador, Bosnia and Uzbekistan — currently winless in the group stage.

trivia_questions.question: In the sweepstake, which participant owns Norway — the team shortening in the odds?
trivia_questions.explanation: Hankin owns Norway, who impressed with a 4-1 win over Iraq at the 2026 World Cup.

trivia_questions.question: Which 2026 World Cup group had Spain, Saudi Arabia, Cape Verde and Uruguay?
trivia_questions.explanation: Spain, Saudi Arabia, Cape Verde and Uruguay were all in Group H.

trivia_questions.question: Which 2026 World Cup group had Belgium, Egypt, Iran and New Zealand?
trivia_questions.explanation: Belgium, Egypt, Iran and New Zealand were all in Group G.

trivia_questions.question: Which 2026 World Cup group had Germany, Curaçao, Ivory Coast and Ecuador?
trivia_questions.explanation: Germany, Curaçao, Ivory Coast and Ecuador were in Group E.

trivia_questions.question: Which 2026 World Cup group had Netherlands, Japan, Sweden and Tunisia?
trivia_questions.explanation: Netherlands, Japan, Sweden and Tunisia were in Group F.

trivia_questions.question: The 2026 World Cup final is scheduled at MetLife Stadium. In which US state is it located?
trivia_questions.explanation: MetLife Stadium is in East Rutherford, New Jersey — just across the Hudson River from New York City.
